title: Twitter @lateinteraction
summary:
I actually remember why we didn't adopt it. Python sucks at multiple *typed* variable assignment.

You can do (A) and (B) below but NOT (C).

(A) a: int = 1
(B) a, b = 1, 2.0
(C) a: int, b: float = 1, 2.0

Why exactly is (C) not allowed, Python?! It's a stupid restriction.

title: Twitter @lateinteraction
summary:
I don't think the way you write LM programs should change, given o1. But I do think the way LM programs are *compiled* does stand to change as o1-style models mature.

Let me explain.

Things that stay the same: modular decomposition along the lines of high-level transformations,

title: Twitter @fchollet
summary:
When I set out to write the 2nd edition of Deep Learning with Python, I thought it would be roughly the same length, and about 50% new content. Now that the draft is done: it's almost 2x longer and it's ~75% new content.

Overall it's a lot more in-depth than the first edition.

title: Twitter @fishnets88
summary:
There's a small python library called "chime" that will play a sound when-ever an error/success happens on a long running task. It's cute but also ... surprisingly useful. It also allows for mario/zelda themed cues.  Made by Max Halford @halford_max 

https://t.co/SrhILhYgeg
